title: lepidium campestre var. errabundum
intro: Lepidium campestre var. errabundum, also known as cress or pepperwort, is a variety of the common peppercress plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a member of the mustard family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lepidium campestre var. errabundum include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and can also be added to salads and used as a garnish for its peppery flavor.
